title: Vegetarian Puff Pastry Pinwheels
description: A delicious and easy-to-make snack or appetizer, perfect for vegetarians. This recipe combines the flaky goodness of puff pastry with the crunch of pak chhana.

ingredients.name: Pak Chhana (Indian-style paneer)
ingredients.notes: available in most Indian grocery stores

instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
2. Roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
3. Spread the pak chhana over the center of the pastry, leaving a 1-inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
4. Sprinkle the cured paprika and garlic powder (if using) over the pak chhana.
5. Brush the edges of the pastry with a little water.
6. Roll the pastry into a tight log, starting from the long side.
7. Cut the log into 1-inch (2.5 cm) thick slices.
8. Place the pinwheels on the prepared baking sheet, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of space between each.
9. Bake for 18-20 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
10. Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes.
